exemplar.title: What is the effect of different kitchen cleaning spray brands, claiming to efficiently remove bacteria, on the growth of E.coli (Escherichia coli) determined by the diameter of the inhibition zone around the paper disc, formed over a 48 hour period?

detailedExemplarData.title: What is the effect of different kitchen cleaning spray brands, claiming to efficiently remove bacteria, on the growth of E.coli (Escherichia coli) determined by the diameter of the inhibition zone around the paper disc, formed over a 48 hour period?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear identification and justification of independent, dependent and controlled variables
Detailed, step-by-step methodology with precise volumes, times and replication strategy
Specific and relevant research question set in a real-world domestic hygiene context
weaknesses: Minor omission of certain control details (e.g., spray concentration consistency after evaporation)
Agar recipe specifics could be stated more precisely
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The research design is of high quality (all strands were placed in band 5), but the necessity to fit the overall total of 11 means scaling down from the raw band-mark of 5/6. The clear context, thorough methodological considerations and reproducible procedure fully justify a strong mid-band mark of 3 out of 6.

strengths: Presentation of raw and processed data is both clear and precise, with units and uncertainties stated
Means, standard deviations and one-way ANOVA are performed correctly and interpreted accurately
Graphical display includes well-labelled error bars
weaknesses: Uncertainty propagation into means and ANOVA is omitted
Minor notation error in formula labelling
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data analysis showed very strong work in communication and processing (two strands at band 5) but with some omissions in uncertainty treatment (one strand at band 4). To reflect its high quality while balancing the fixed total, a score of 3 out of 6 is appropriate.

strengths: Conclusion is directly tied to the data and hypothesis testing, correctly rejecting Hâ
Comparison to scientific literature on BAC and bacterial resistance adds depth and context
weaknesses: Some repetitive phrasing and minor clarity issues in stating the statistical decision
Could more explicitly detail the F > Fcrit logic
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion meets top-band descriptors in both relevance and contextual comparison (band 5), but to align with the total mark constraint it is scaled to a mid-range score of 3 out of 6, reflecting both its strengths and minor lapses.

strengths: Identifies specific methodological weaknesses and their impact on bias
Proposes realistic improvements that address identified limitations
weaknesses: Impact explanations are descriptive rather than fully analytical
Improvement rationales lack depth in explaining how they enhance reliability and validity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation was the weakest area (one strand at band 4, one at band 3). To reflect its lower standing relative to the other criteria and to satisfy the fixed total, a score of 2 out of 6 is awarded.

strandSelections.reasoning: The conclusion directly cites the mean inhibition zones and ANOVA outcome, explicitly rejecting the null hypothesis and identifying Brand A as most effective.  **Statements align fully with the processed data**, but some wording is repetitive and precision could improve, hence 5/6.

Raw data for every side of every inhibition zone are tabulated with units and stated measurement uncertainty; processed means, SDs and a clearly labelled graph with error bars are provided.  The presentation is **both clear (well organised, labelled) and precise (mm to 2 dp, units stated)**, though some tables suffer from cramped formatting, so 5/6 is justified.

Means, standard deviations and a one-way ANOVA are performed correctly; calculations match the raw data and are interpreted accurately to test the hypothesis.  **Processing is appropriate, error-free and sufficient to address the question**, though the student does not provide post-hoc tests, so 5 rather than 6 marks is warranted.

Specific weaknesses are identifiedâoverlapping zones causing measurement difficulty, limited temperature, single bacterial speciesâand the student comments on how each could bias results, demonstrating **description of impact rather than a thorough explanation**.  This aligns with the upper value of the 3-4 band.

The student compares findings to published information on BAC concentration, molecular weight differences and Gram-negative resistance, providing multiple literature citations.  The discussion **goes beyond superficial statements and uses scientific concepts to justify the observed ranking**, meeting the top-band descriptor; minor depth gaps merit 5 rather than 6.

The research question is narrowly focused on the comparative antibacterial efficacy of four named commercial sprays against E. coli, and the introduction provides detailed, product-specific chemical background (BAC, DDAC, lactic acid, KOH).  This establishes a **specific, real-world context (domestic hygiene after the COVID-19 pandemic)** and links the biology of Gram-negative bacteria to the investigation.  Because the context is both appropriate and explicitly tied to the variables under study, the work meets the descriptor for the top band, though minor repetition and some citation lapses prevent awarding the full 6 marks.

Instrument uncertainties are given (Â±0.5 mm, Â±0.5 cmÂ³) and quoted in raw-data headings; standard deviations are calculated and displayed as error bars.  However, **no combined or propagated uncertainty for the means is presented, nor are uncertainties incorporated into the ANOVA**.  This represents âevidence with significant omissions,â fitting the 3-4 band; the treatment is competent enough for the upper value of 4.

Improvement suggestions include increasing disc spacing to avoid overlap and testing other bacteria.  While relevant, **the rationale for how these changes would specifically enhance reliability or validity is only briefly stated**, fitting a descriptive rather than explanatory levelâhence the lower mark in the 3-4 band.

The student identifies independent, dependent and controlled variables in a structured table, justifying each control and explaining how it is maintained (e.g., incubation temperature limited by IB safety rules, disc soaking time, agar composition).  **Rationales show why each factor affects bacterial growth and how the chosen procedure minimises confounding effects**, which goes beyond merely listing them.  While thorough, some controls (e.g., spray concentration consistency after evaporation) are not considered, so a score of 5 rather than 6 is appropriate.

The step-by-step method includes exact volumes (10 cmÂ³), times (5 min impregnation, 48 h incubation), disc diameter (15 Â± 0.5 mm), temperatures (26 Â°C) and replication strategy (5 trials).  Photos of key stages reinforce clarity.  **Another investigator could reproduce the work with negligible ambiguity**; only minor omissions (e.g., precise agar recipe) remain, justifying 5 marks in the top band.

annotations.comment: Figure 4 effectively illustrates aseptic technique for culture transfer, aiding clarity in methodological reproduction.
Degrees of freedom calculation is correct, but the total N should specify that 5 trials Ã4 measurements Ã5 groups =100 observations.

The research question is clearly stated within a specific domestic hygiene context, linking E. coli growth to inhibition zone diameter. This provides focus and realâworld relevance. Consider adjusting minor repetition for conciseness.
The introduction repeats pandemic context unnecessarily and lacks consistent citation format. Streamline background statements and verify all references follow a single citation style.
Background on Gram-negative cell walls is thorough, but the text could better link these structural details to expected inhibitor mechanisms in the disc diffusion assay.
The description of E. coli growth conditions is precise, supporting reproducibility by clarifying optimal temperature and media preferences.
The section on antibacterial agents provides detailed chemical actions, justifying the choice of sprays; very clear mechanistic context.
The hypotheses are clearly articulated with null and alternative statements; including predicted direction enhances testability.
Table summarizing product agents is well organized and directly informs the experimental variables, enhancing methodological transparency.
Preliminary testing rationale is sound but would benefit from quantitative details on variability observed at 24 vs 48âh to justify the chosen incubation period.
Controlled variables are comprehensively listed with justifications and methods, demonstrating attention to confounding factors.
Consider adding a control for evaporation of volatile components (e.g. ethanol), as changes in concentration during soaking could affect disc potency.
Equipment list is exhaustive and specifies quantities, supporting reproducibility by another investigator.
The risk assessment is detailed and includes microbiological safety, demonstrating thorough consideration of procedural hazards.
Ensure that the plate division into zones does not bias disc placement; randomizing zone assignment could reduce positional effects.
Raw data table is comprehensive with measurement uncertainties stated (Â±0.5âmm); presentation is precise and transparent.
Consider adding a separate column for control data summary rather than repeating 0.01 values to improve readability and emphasize negligible inhibition.
The formula incorrectly labels the mean as $$\frac{1}{x}$$; revise to use standard notation $$\bar{x}=\frac{\sum x_i}{n}$$.
Processed results table clearly presents means and SD; both communication clarity and precision are strong.
Graph interpretation mentions overlapping error bars; consider annotating statistical significance directly on the graph for clearer visual impact.
Justification for oneâway ANOVA is well argued, correctly explaining why a tâtest would be inappropriate for comparing >2 groups.
ANOVA summary table is accurately computed; Pâvalue and F observed are consistent with raw data analysis.
The F critical values table is appropriately referenced and correctly used to obtain $$F_{crit}=2.47$$ at (4,95) df.
The conclusion reaffirms the experimental aim and links results to hypothesis, demonstrating relevance to the research question.
Comparison with literature on BAC concentration and molecular weight provides valuable scientific context to justify observed brand ranking.
Some concluding paragraphs are overly verbose. Condense repetitive explanations to improve precision and readability.
Measurement of four points per zone instead of maximum diameter is a methodological strength, enhancing the validity of inhibition zone data.
The conclusion statement ambiguously describes rejecting Hâ; explicitly state âobserved F > Fcrit, therefore Hâ is rejectedâ to ensure clarity.
Overlapping zones are identified as a weakness; quantify how often this occurred or estimate the measurement error to explain its impact further.
The proposed extension to test varying bacterial species is highly relevant and addresses limitations in generalizing results across microorganisms.
Bibliography entries are numerous but inconsistent in style (e.g., punctuation, italics). Adopt a single referencing format for uniformity and clarity.

strands.name: Methodology description

strands.name: Research question context
strands.content: The research question is outlined within a broad context.

strands.name: Methodological considerations

strands.name: Methodology description
strands.content: The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced with few ambiguities or omissions.

strands.name: Research question context
strands.content: The research question is described within a specific and appropriate context.

strands.name: Methodological considerations
strands.content: Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are explained.

strands.content: The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: Some processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with major omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of an appropriate consideration of uncertainties.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out appropriately and accurately.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is stated that is relevant to the research question but is not supported by the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: The conclusion makes superficial comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is described that is relevant to the research question but is not fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is described that makes some relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Improvements

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report states generic methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation are stated.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report describes specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are described.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report explains the relative impact of specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
